user_query:
I’m preparing to launch an AI assistant for game development teams and need a research-informed plan plus meeting documentation. Please read the following files under input/:
- input/research_topic.txt — the research question and any constraints (e.g., year range)
- input/company_brief.md — a short product brief
- input/meeting.txt — messy notes from a recent internal sync (includes date)
- input/gamedevbench_abstract.txt — the abstract of a relevant benchmark paper

Deliverables (write all outputs exactly to the specified paths under output/):
1) Literature review: Use the research topic and constraints to conduct an academic literature review. Include at least 12 peer‑reviewed works, identify themes, and provide a structured bibliography with DOIs. Save as output/research/literature_review.md. Required structure:
   - Title line starting with "# Literature Review: ..."
   - "## Overview" section with scope (count of papers) and open access count if known
   - "### Most Cited Works" (at least 5 entries)
   - "## Thematic Analysis" with named themes and short annotated bullets per paper
   - "## Full Bibliography" with one item per paper, each including DOI (when available)
2) Top papers JSON: A machine‑readable list of the 5 most influential/central papers you found, including title, authors (array), year (number), citations (number), and doi (string or null). Save as output/research/top5.json.
3) Benchmark abstract summary: Write an 80–200 word standalone paragraph summarizing the abstract provided in input/gamedevbench_abstract.txt (mention the paper title explicitly). Save as output/research/gamedevbench_summary.txt.
4) GTM plan: Based on insights from the literature review and the company brief, produce a concise go‑to‑market plan for the first 12 weeks. Include the following sections and constraints: Target Customer Profile, Positioning Statement, Competitive Landscape, Channel Strategy Matrix (score channels and then clearly list Selected Channels limited to 2–3 with one quick‑win ≤30 days and one compounding ≥6 months), Pricing & Packaging (tiers and value metric), Launch Timeline (Weeks 1–12 with checklist items), Success Metrics (leading/lagging), and explicit Kill Criteria that include a 60‑day channel kill rule. Save as output/gtm/gtm_plan.md.
5) Meeting notes: Extract and organize the meeting content from input/meeting.txt into a clean notes file. Include Summary (2–3 sentences), Action Items with owners and deadlines in checklist form, Decisions, Open Questions (if any), Next Steps, and a collapsible Raw Notes section containing the original text. Use the exact filename output/meeting-notes/2026-04-18_project-sync.md (date from the notes; keep this name). Ensure the file uses UTF‑8 and the checklist format "- [ ] @Owner: Task — Deadline".

Notes:
- Use recent peer‑reviewed sources and include DOIs in the literature review and bibliography where available.
- Keep everything in English and plain text/Markdown/JSON only.
- Do not place any files outside the specified output/ paths.
